claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine whether you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess this, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children.

Let me start with a basic question:

**How many children are you inquiring about, and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in finding out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my daughter Alex, who's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'm assessing your eligibility for Child Benefit for your daughter Alex.

Before I can make a determination, I need to understand your family situation better. Let me ask you:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful to know.

Now I need to gather a bit more information to properly assess your eligibility:

**Are you a resident of the UK, and do you have the right to reside in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and have the right to reside here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you. I need to check one more important thing:

**Have you been living in the UK for at least 2 of the last 3 years?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have been living in the UK for at least 2 of the last 3 years.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that. Just to clarify the final detail:

**Is Alex under 16 years old, or is she 16 or over but still in full-time education or an approved training scheme?**

(I know you mentioned she's 8, so I'm expecting the answer is that she's under 16, but I need to confirm this for the assessment.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - she's 8 years old, so she's under 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! I now have all the information I need to make a determination. Let me consult the eligibility rules to ensure I'm applying them correctly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the eligibility rules I've reviewed and the information you've provided, I can now make my determination:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Excellent news!** You are **eligible for Child Benefit** for your daughter Alex.

You meet all the key eligibility criteria:
- Alex is under 16 years old
- She lives with you
- You are responsible for her
- You are a UK resident with the right to reside
- You have been in the UK for at least 2 of the last 3 years

You can now proceed with making your Child Benefit claim through the official government channels. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: (1) She is under 16 years old; (2) She lives with you in the UK; (3) You are responsible for her; (4) You are a UK resident with the right to reside in the UK; and (5) You have been living in the UK for at least 2 of the last 3 years. All conditions under the primary eligibility rules are satisfied.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for your daughter Alex. You meet all the necessary criteria: you're responsible for a child under 16 who lives with you, you're a UK resident with the right to reside, and you've been in the UK for at least 2 of the last 3 years. You can proceed with your Child Benefit claim."

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: (1) She is under 16 years old; (2) She lives with you in the UK; (3) You are responsible for her; (4) You are a UK resident with the right to reside in the UK; and (5) You have been living in the UK for at least 2 of the last 3 years. All conditions under the primary eligibility rules are satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for your daughter Alex. You meet all the necessary criteria: you're responsible for a child under 16 who lives with you, you're a UK resident with the right to reside, and you've been in the UK for at least 2 of the last 3 years. You can proceed with your Child Benefit claim."
